title: User Login and Token Authentication Flow

login_flow: LoginRouter → EdgeRateLimitService: check('login', true) — 429 if exceeded
LoginRouter → Database: get_user({username}) or get_user({email}) — 400 if not found
AuthService → AuthService: create_session_(user, meta) — extracts ip, user_agent, referer, origin from req
SessionService → Database: INSERT INTO sessions (uuid, user_id, meta, last_activity, created_at)
SessionService → Redis: SET session-cache:{uuid} JSON(session) EX 300
SessionService returns session {uuid, user_uid, user_id, meta}
AuthService → TokenService: sign('auth', {type:'session', uuid, user_uid}) → session_token (JWT)
AuthService → TokenService: sign('auth', {type:'gui', uuid, user_uid}) → gui_token (JWT)
complete_ → Client: res.cookie(config.cookie_name, session_token, {httpOnly:true, secure:true, sameSite:'none'})
token_auth_flow: Client → configurable_auth: request with token (body.auth_token | Authorization header | cookie | query.auth_token | socket)
configurable_auth: extract token from request source (body > header > cookie > query > socket)
AuthService → TokenService: verify('auth', token) → decoded JWT payload
[cache miss]: SessionService → Database: SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE uuid=? — tryHardRead
SessionService → Redis: SET session-cache:{uuid} (pending flush) + SADD flush-pending-set
AuthService returns Actor(UserActorType{user, session:uuid, hasHttpOnlyCookie: type==='session'})
configurable_auth: req.actor = actor; req.user = user; req.token = token
legacy_token_upgrade: configurable_auth catches LegacyTokenError (token has no 'type' field) on /whoami
AuthService → TokenService: verify('auth', cur_token) → decoded (legacy format with .uuid)
configurable_auth → Client: res.cookie(config.cookie_name, session_token, {httpOnly:true, secure:true, sameSite:'none'})
get_user_app_token: AuthService: actor = Context.get('actor') — must be UserActorType or throws 403
AuthService → TokenService: sign('auth', {type:'app-under-user', user_uid, app_uid, session:uuid_fpe.encrypt(session)}) → JWT
get_public_hosted_actor_cookie_name: AuthService returns configured name or DEFAULT_PUBLIC_HOSTED_ACTOR_COOKIE_NAME
session_flush_loop: SessionService → Redis: SET flush-lock:{uuid} EX 30 NX (skip if lock not acquired)
SessionService → Database: UPDATE sessions SET meta=?, last_activity=? WHERE uuid=?
SessionService → Database: UPDATE user SET last_activity_ts=? WHERE id=?

key_relationships: Actor uses UserActorType | AppUnderUserActorType | AccessTokenActorType | SiteActorType

token_types.session: HTTP-only cookie token; hasHttpOnlyCookie=true; allows user-protected endpoints
token_types.gui: Response-body token; hasHttpOnlyCookie=false; client-side use only
token_types.app-under-user: App acting on behalf of user; includes encrypted session UUID
token_types.access-token: Long-lived API token; references authorizer actor
token_types.actor-site: Site-level token; references puter site by site_uid
token_types.otp: Short-lived (5m) token for MFA step; signed with 'otp' namespace

important_notes: Two-token architecture: session_token (httpOnly cookie, for protected endpoints) + gui_token (response body, for client JS)
authenticate_from_token dispatches on JWT 'type' field; missing 'type' → LegacyTokenError (upgrade path)
Session UUIDs in app-under-user tokens are Format-Preserving Encrypted (UUIDFPE) to avoid exposure
check_session handles legacy token upgrade on /whoami — creates new session, returns GUI token
OTP path: login returns 202 with otp_jwt_token; client POSTs to /login/otp with code
Rate limiting via EdgeRateLimitService.check('login') on every login attempt
Session flush to DB is async, distributed-locked, random-interval (5–60s)
/get-gui-token endpoint exchanges session cookie for GUI token; /session/sync-cookie syncs cookie after user switch
